metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4539
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 11A: To examine the subcellular localization of SYT7, an RFP tag was introduced at the 3'-end of the endogenous Syt7 locus using CRISPR.
Figure S11A: A similar sfGFP version (SYT7GFP) was also generated with CRISPR that showed the same intra-terminal expression pattern as SYT7RFP.
Figure 11CD: Immunostaining of 3rd instar larvae with anti-RFP antisera revealed SYT7RFP was enriched in presynaptic terminals and formed an expansive tubular network near the plasma membrane that extended into the centre of the bouton.
Figure S11: Neuronal knockdown of Syt7 with two independent RNAi lines (elavC155-GAL4; UAS-Syt7 RNAi) dramatically reduced SYT7RFP on western blots (Figure 11B) and eliminated expression of SYT7RFP at the NMJ indicating the signal is specific to SYT7.

Figure 12: To further characterize the subsynaptic localization of SYT7, fluorescently-tagged compartmental markers or compartment-specific antisera were used for labelling in the Syt7RFP background. Majority of the SYT7 fluorescence colocalises with per-AZ protein dynamin and partially colocalises with presynaptic t-SNARE protein SYX1. SYT7 fluorescence also surrounded the active zone marker BRP indicative of a peri-AZ pattern of localisation.

SYT7 has a transmembrane domain as depicted in Q9V4C4_DROME. It also localised with the neuronal membrane marker HRP in Figure 11. Based on this, the term "integral component..." was chosen.

As the cytofluorograms of the SYT7 fluorescence with that of the presynaptic, active zone, peri-active zone marker proteins has a lot of variabilities, the term "integral component of presynaptic membrane" was chosen.. Experimental description: To generate SYT7RFP, a donor plasmid that flanked RFP and a DsRed cassette was generated from the pScarless plasmid (courtesy of Kate O'Connor-Giles) with 1 Kb homology arms from the 3' end of the Syt7 gene.
